pre_infusion_safety.fatal_alerts: High Cytokine Release Syndrome (CRS) risk. Profound prolonged T-cell depletion.
mandatory_checks: Verify active infection prophylaxis (Bactrim/Acyclovir)
Confirm premedications are administered 30 mins prior

extravasation_management.action: Monitor IV patency. Manage per standard irritant protocol.

during_infusion.monitoring_frequency: Vitals every 15-30 minutes during infusion (high CRS risk).
stop_criteria: Severe hypotension or cardiac instability
Anaphylaxis, severe hypersensitivity, or uncontrolled rigors

nursing_assessment: Assess for delayed CRS symptoms
Monitor for opportunistic infections (CMV, PCP)
post_infusion_care.patient_education: Report fever, severe chills, or difficulty breathing immediately.

report_to_physician: Hypotension/Tachycardia
Signs of active infection

pre_infusion_safety.fatal_alerts: High risk of Differentiation Syndrome (DS). Severe teratogenicity.
mandatory_checks: Check current WBC count (prophylactic steroids often ordered if >10k)
Verify negative pregnancy status

extravasation_management.action: Ensure capsules are swallowed whole; do not crush.

during_infusion.monitoring_frequency: Daily assessment for fluid status and respiratory changes.
stop_criteria: Acute respiratory distress (suspected DS)
Unexplained sudden weight gain or edema

nursing_assessment: Assess lung sounds and daily weights strictly
Monitor for severe headache (pseudotumor cerebri)
post_infusion_care.patient_education: Take with food. Do not take vitamin A supplements.

report_to_physician: Shortness of breath or unexplained fever (DS alert)
Severe, unremitting headache
Rapid weight gain > 1-2 kg/day
manage_at_bedside: Dry skin and lips (apply emollients heavily)

pre_infusion_safety.fatal_alerts: High risk of fatal QTc interval prolongation and Differentiation Syndrome.
mandatory_checks: Verify baseline/recent ECG (QTc < 460 ms)
Ensure Potassium > 4.0 mEq/L and Magnesium > 2.0 mg/dL

extravasation_management.action: Ensure free-flowing IV. Monitor for local inflammation.

during_infusion.monitoring_frequency: Continuous tele/ECG monitoring if high risk; frequent vitals.
stop_criteria: QTc prolongation > 500 ms
Onset of arrhythmias or syncope

nursing_assessment: Monitor continuous cardiac rhythm and strict I/O
Assess for signs of Differentiation Syndrome (dyspnea, weight gain)
post_infusion_care.patient_education: Report palpitations, dizziness, or shortness of breath instantly.

report_to_physician: Syncope or irregular heartbeat
Shortness of breath with fever
Electrolytes dropping below target parameters

pre_infusion_safety.fatal_alerts: High risk of severe coagulopathy (hemorrhage/thrombosis), pancreatitis, and anaphylaxis.
mandatory_checks: Verify Fibrinogen level is > 1.0 g/L (or > 100 mg/dL)
Ensure emergency anaphylaxis kit is at bedside

extravasation_management.action: Stop infusion. Do not flush violently if extravasation is suspected.

during_infusion.monitoring_frequency: Vitals q15 mins for first hour. Direct observation for hypersensitivity.
stop_criteria: Signs of anaphylaxis (urticaria, wheezing, hypotension)
Sudden severe abdominal pain (pancreatitis alert)

nursing_assessment: Monitor amylase/lipase and blood glucose
Perform strict neuro checks (risk of cerebral sinus thrombosis)
post_infusion_care.patient_education: Report severe stomach pain, sudden headaches, or unusual bleeding.

report_to_physician: Severe abdominal pain radiating to back
Sudden neurological deficits or severe headache
Hypersensitivity reactions
manage_at_bedside: Monitor hyperglycemia (administer insulin sliding scale as ordered)

pre_infusion_safety.fatal_alerts: Drug is highly unstable once reconstituted (administer within 1-4 hours based on temperature).
mandatory_checks: Verify administration timing limits with pharmacy
Check current CBC for severe neutropenia/thrombocytopenia

extravasation_management.action: If SubQ, rotate sites. If IV, verify brisk blood return.

during_infusion.monitoring_frequency: Standard vitals pre and post administration.
stop_criteria: Acute hypersensitivity (rare but possible)
Severe pain at IV insertion site

nursing_assessment: Assess injection sites for severe erythema or necrosis
Monitor bowel habits (high risk of constipation with prophylactic anti-emetics)
post_infusion_care.patient_education: Expect local redness at injection sites; apply cold compresses for comfort.

report_to_physician: Fever > 38.0°C (neutropenic sepsis risk)
Bleeding or severe petechiae
Injection site showing signs of active infection/necrosis

pre_infusion_safety.fatal_alerts: Risk of severe Tumor Lysis Syndrome (TLS) and secondary viral infections (opportunistic).
mandatory_checks: Confirm active viral prophylaxis (Acyclovir/Valacyclovir)
Ensure aggressive hydration orders are active

extravasation_management.action: Stop immediately. Aspirate residual. Apply COLD compresses.
extravasation_management.antidote: Sodium Thiosulfate (if protocol specifies for severe cases)

during_infusion.monitoring_frequency: Observe closely for skin reactions/anaphylaxis during infusion.
stop_criteria: Loss of blood return or pain at IV site
Development of acute rash or respiratory distress

nursing_assessment: Monitor renal function, uric acid, and potassium (TLS watch)
Assess for progressive skin rashes (Stevens-Johnson risk)
post_infusion_care.patient_education: Maintain high oral fluid intake for 48 hours post-infusion.

report_to_physician: New onset peeling skin rash or mucosal lesions
Decreased urine output or flank pain
Signs of extravasation

pre_infusion_safety.fatal_alerts: NEVER FLUSH THE IV LINE (fatal bolus risk). High risk of Cytokine Release Syndrome (CRS) and Neurotoxicity (ICANS).
mandatory_checks: Confirm Dexamethasone given strictly prior to first dose/step-ups
Verify dedicated continuous IV line without any other concurrent meds

extravasation_management.action: Ensure line is secure. Do not manipulate the pump aggressively.

during_infusion.monitoring_frequency: Vitals q4h. ICE (neuro) score q4-8h minimum.
stop_criteria: Any change in baseline neuro status (tremors, confusion, aphasia)
Fever > 38.5°C accompanied by hypotension (CRS)

nursing_assessment: Maintain strict continuous neurologic evaluations
Monitor liver enzymes daily
post_infusion_care.patient_education: Do not manipulate the IV pump. Request help for any bathroom trips.

report_to_physician: Deterioration in handwriting or ICE score (ICANS)
New onset tremors, seizures, or aphasia
Signs of severe CRS (Hypoxia, high fever, hypotension)
manage_at_bedside: Low-grade fever (administer scheduled antipyretics and monitor)
Mild headache (if neuro assessment is otherwise stable)

pre_infusion_safety.fatal_alerts: High risk of Herpes Zoster (Shingles) reactivation and severe peripheral neuropathy.
mandatory_checks: Confirm active antiviral prophylaxis is prescribed and taken
Perform baseline neuropathy assessment prior to every dose

extravasation_management.action: SubQ route preferred to minimize vascular risk. If IV, ensure free flow.

during_infusion.monitoring_frequency: Check vitals pre and post dose for orthostatic changes.
stop_criteria: Acute severe orthostatic hypotension drops
Severe acute worsening of neuropathic pain

nursing_assessment: Assess for autonomic neuropathy (bowel motility, orthostasis)
Monitor site for local reaction (SubQ common)
post_infusion_care.patient_education: Sit on edge of bed before standing. Report new numbness or tingling.

report_to_physician: New or worsening burning, numbness, or pain in hands/feet
Severe dizziness upon standing (syncope risk)
Signs of shingles rash
manage_at_bedside: Mild orthostasis (encourage slow position changes and hydration)

pre_infusion_safety.fatal_alerts: Severe hemorrhagic cystitis risk. Dose-dependent fatal cardiotoxicity (necrosis/CHF).
mandatory_checks: Verify robust hydration orders (Target 3L/day total fluid)
Confirm Mesna availability and dosing times (if high-dose protocol)

extravasation_management.action: Stop infusion if pain occurs. Monitor for local phlebitis.

during_infusion.monitoring_frequency: Vitals q1h during high-dose infusions. Strict I/O monitoring.
stop_criteria: Gross visible hematuria in Foley or urinal
Signs of acute heart failure (rapid weight gain, crackles, shortness of breath)

nursing_assessment: Mandatory strict I/O. Maintain high urine output.
Assess urine for blood (dipstick if suspected)
post_infusion_care.patient_education: Drink lots of fluids. You MUST wake up in the night to empty your bladder.

report_to_physician: Pink, red, or bloody urine
Dysuria (burning during urination)
Chest pain, rapid heartbeat, or sudden shortness of breath

pre_infusion_safety.fatal_alerts: High-dose (HiDAC) causes severe irreversible cerebellar toxicity and chemical conjunctivitis.
mandatory_checks: Perform baseline cerebellar neuro check (signature/writing test) prior to every dose
Verify Dexamethasone eye drops are actively administered

during_infusion.monitoring_frequency: Continuous neuro checks per protocol. Check eyes for redness.
stop_criteria: Any change in cerebellar function (nystagmus, dysmetria, ataxia, slurred speech)
Acute severe eye pain

nursing_assessment: Monitor for 'Cytarabine Syndrome' (fever, maculopapular rash, bone pain) 6-12h post-dose
Administer scheduled eye drops faithfully (continue 24-48h post final dose)
post_infusion_care.patient_education: Report any blurriness of vision, feeling off-balance, or gritty feeling in eyes.

report_to_physician: Worsening handwriting or inability to walk straight (Ataxia)
Severe eye burning or tearing despite drops
Fever with severe bone pain
manage_at_bedside: Administer eye drops precisely on schedule

pre_infusion_safety.fatal_alerts: CRITICAL VESICANT. Deep tissue necrosis on extravasation. Lifetime cumulative cardiotoxicity.
mandatory_checks: Verify baseline ECHO/MUGA LVEF is acceptable
Verify central line patency with brisk, flawless blood return

extravasation_management.action: STOP IMMEDIATELY. Do not flush. Aspirate residual. Apply COLD PACKS.

during_infusion.monitoring_frequency: Direct nurse presence required during entire push/infusion.
stop_criteria: ANY loss of blood return, resistance to push, or patient report of pain/burning
Sudden cardiac arrhythmias

nursing_assessment: Assess oral mucosa closely for severe mucositis
Monitor cardiac rhythm post-infusion
post_infusion_care.patient_education: Your urine will turn red/orange for 1-2 days. Use soft toothbrush.

report_to_physician: Signs of extravasation (swelling, redness, pain at site)
Palpitations, sudden edema, or shortness of breath (Cardiotoxicity)
Fever in presence of severe mucositis
manage_at_bedside: Provide aggressive ice chips (cryotherapy) during infusion to prevent mucositis

pre_infusion_safety.fatal_alerts: Severe T-cell depletion. Fatal Transfusion-Associated Graft-Versus-Host Disease (TA-GVHD) risk.
mandatory_checks: CRITICAL: All transfused blood products MUST be irradiated
Verify PJP (Bactrim) and Antiviral (Acyclovir) prophylaxis are active

extravasation_management.action: Standard irritant care. Ensure patent line.

during_infusion.monitoring_frequency: Routine vitals. Observe for acute neuro changes.
stop_criteria: Sudden acute altered mental status, visual changes, or seizures
Severe hypersensitivity

nursing_assessment: Monitor for profound delayed myelosuppression and opportunistic infections
Assess for autoimmune hemolytic anemia (jaundice, sudden hemoglobin drop)
post_infusion_care.patient_education: You are at high risk for infection for months after this drug. Avoid sick contacts.

report_to_physician: Visual disturbances, severe confusion, or coma (Neurotoxicity)
Signs of anemia (extreme fatigue, pale skin, jaundice)
Any signs of infection or fever
manage_at_bedside: Ensure blood bank is alerted to "Irradiated Only" status

pre_infusion_safety.fatal_alerts: Severe bone marrow suppression. Extreme TLS risk upon rapid debulking of WBC.
mandatory_checks: Confirm hazardous drug handling precautions (wear chemo gloves for oral prep)
Verify Allopurinol/Hydration ordered if starting for massive leukocytosis

during_infusion.monitoring_frequency: Daily CBC monitoring during initial debulking phase.
stop_criteria: WBC drops below designated safety parameters rapidly
Severe TLS lab derangements

nursing_assessment: Assess lower extremities for painful, non-healing leg ulcers
Monitor renal function and uric acid
post_infusion_care.patient_education: Wash hands after handling. Do not open capsules. Use sun protection.

report_to_physician: Development of painful skin ulcers on legs/ankles
Rapid decrease in urine output
Signs of severe anemia or infection
manage_at_bedside: Mild nausea (take with food if acceptable)

pre_infusion_safety.fatal_alerts: NEVER INTRATHECAL. Fatal if given spinally. Must be dispensed in a minibag to prevent accidental IV push errors.
mandatory_checks: Verify patient ID strictly
Confirm IV patency with brisk blood return (Potent Vesicant)
premeds: Laxatives (prophylactic bowel regimen to prevent fatal paralytic ileus)

extravasation_management.action: Stop infusion immediately. Aspirate. Apply WARM COMPRESSES ONLY.

during_infusion.monitoring_frequency: Continuous direct observation and line assessment during administration.
stop_criteria: Patient complains of burning/pain at IV site
Any signs of redness, swelling, or loss of blood return

nursing_assessment: Assess for constipation and strict bowel sounds (paralytic ileus risk)
Check for severe neurotoxicity (foot drop, severe jaw pain, loss of deep tendon reflexes)
post_infusion_care.patient_education: Report numbness in fingers/toes, jaw pain, or lack of bowel movements immediately.

report_to_physician: Signs of paralytic ileus (no bowel movement, severe bloating)
New onset foot drop or loss of motor function
Severe jaw pain
